mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening supply chain that enhances productivity and reduces costs for S&P 500 companies, producing positive forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold strategy over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that increases wage pressure and reduces corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to outperform a sell-and-hold strategy over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts signals a strengthening demand for residential construction that enhances economic growth and increases corporate profitability, producing positive forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to outperform a momentum strategy over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 4-month increase in real disposable personal income signals a strengthening demand for consumer goods that enhances economic growth and increases corporate profitability, producing positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to outperform a value strategy over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a weakening labor market that reduces wage pressure and increases corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening labor periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to outperform a growth strategy over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the consumer price index signals a strengthening monetary policy that reduces inflation expectations and increases corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening monetary periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to outperform a dividend strategy over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 5-month decline in real GDP signals a weakening supply chain that reduces productivity and increases costs for S&P 500 companies, producing negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to outperform a short-selling strategy over any 3-year rolling window.
